headline: Valero Energy Earnings Skyrocket As Crack Spreads Soar Amid Iran War
summary: Valero Energy easily beat first-quarter earnings estimates early Thursday, after the Iran war caused refining margins to surge.  Valero stock fell slightly after rallying into results.  Fellow refiner Phillips 66 posted an earnings surprise on Wednesday, due to higher refining margins.

headline: Following UAE’s OPEC Exit Shock, Diamondback Energy Could Be the Smartest Bet Now
summary: The UAE’s decision to exit OPEC/OPEC+ has injected new uncertainty into oil markets and pushed crude prices above $100 per barrel. Low-cost Permian operator Diamondback Energy may be positioned as one of the clearest near-term beneficiaries.

headline: The Big OPEC Breakup Could Mean Lower Oil Prices, More Volatility and New Regional Alliances
summary: The United Arab Emirates exited OPEC, President Donald Trump is preparing for a long blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, and there’s no peace deal in sight.  Today I’m diving into what a weaker OPEC means for investors, how Gulf states are changing their sovereign wealth fund playbook, and Indonesia’s natural gas ambitions.  Where Does Oil Go From Here?

headline: Exxon Mobil Lawsuit Tests Orphaned Well Accounting And ESG Risk Perceptions
summary: A new lawsuit alleges Exxon Mobil and Empire Petroleum committed accounting fraud related to orphaned well cleanup costs in New Mexico. The case centers on retirement obligations for 670 oil and gas wells and whether cleanup liabilities were understated. The outcome could influence who ultimately bears the cost for remediation, including public entities and taxpayers. For investors tracking Exxon Mobil, NYSE:XOM, the lawsuit comes at a time when the stock trades around $154.67 and has a...
